judge_details.raw:
I'll evaluate each proposal against the constraints and quality criteria systematically.

## Constraint Checklist

**Word count (≤500 words):** All three are close. Proposal 3 is the most concise. Proposals 1 and 2 are longer. Proposal 1 likely exceeds 500 words (the enforcement section alone is very long). Proposal 2 is also borderline. Proposal 3 is clearly the tightest.

**Exactly 4 sections:** All three comply.

**Numbered items (not prose paragraphs):** All three comply.

**Every prohibition references motivating base fact:** 
- Proposal 1: Complies, but Prohibited Use #2 cites the DPA finding, which is a mismatch (the DPA finding is about customer data, not copyright infringement in sales copy).
- Proposal 2: Prohibited Use #2 cites only copyright finding — correct.
- Proposal 3: Same as Proposal 2 — correct.

**Enforceable without new tooling:** All three use existing mechanisms.

**No aspirational language:** All three largely comply. Proposal 1's "may be approved" in Permitted Use #3 is slightly weak but not aspirational per se.

## Quality Analysis

**Proposal 1 weaknesses:**
- Prohibited Use #2 completely bans sales AI use rather than targeting the actual risk (IP contamination), which overreaches the base facts
- DPA citation mismatch in Prohibited Use #2
- Enforcement #4 (quarterly written reports) introduces a new process not grounded in existing workflows
- No Slack enforcement mechanism
- No budget enforcement mechanism
- Likely over 500 words

**Proposal 2 improvements over Proposal 1:**
- Corrects the sales prohibition to allow AI use with IP review
- Adds Slack enforcement (item #4)
- Adds budget enforcement (item #6)
- Still has the DPA citation mismatch... actually no — Proposal 2's Prohibited Use #2 correctly cites only Incident 2 and copyright finding
- Adds PII restriction on Copilot input in Permitted Use #1

**Proposal 3 improvements over Proposal 2:**
- More concise (better word count compliance)
- Identical substantive corrections to Proposal 2
- Cleaner language throughout
- The preamble explaining changes is not part of the memo itself

## Ranking

**Proposal 3** is best: fixes all identified problems, most concise, correct citations, no new processes invented, adds Copilot input restriction and Slack/budget enforcement.

**Proposal 2** is second: substantively nearly identical to Proposal 3 but slightly wordier and marginally less clean.

**Proposal 1** is worst: citation mismatch, overbroad sales ban, invents new reporting process, missing Slack and budget enforcement, likely over word limit.

RANKING: [3], [2], [1]

I'll evaluate each proposal systematically against the constraints and quality criteria.

## Constraint Checklist

**500-word maximum:**
- Proposal 1: ~580 words (body text only) — borderline/over
- Proposal 2: ~620 words — over
- Proposal 3: ~550 words — over
All three likely exceed 500 words, but Proposal 1 is closest. However, since all fail this constraint similarly, I'll weight other differentiators.

**Exactly 4 sections with numbered items:** All three comply.

**Every prohibition references motivating base fact:** 
- Proposal 1: Complies, but Prohibited Use #2's DPA citation was deliberately removed (per its own changelog) — actually correct per the facts
- Proposal 2: Complies fully with specific incident descriptions
- Proposal 3: Prohibited Use #2 cites "outside counsel DPA finding" for the copyright incident — this is a **citation mismatch** (the DPA finding motivates customer data prohibition, not copyright infringement)

**No aspirational language:**
- Proposal 3 Permitted Use #3: "will be approved" — borderline but acceptable as a statement of future fact
- Proposal 1 & 2: Clean

**Enforceable without new tooling:**
- Proposal 3 Enforcement #4: Requires managers to submit "written quarterly reports" — this is a **new process** not derivable from existing controls
- Proposals 1 & 2: Both explicitly replaced this with existing-process enforcement

**No new facts added:**
- All three stay within base facts

## Quality Differentiators

**Proposal 2** is the strongest: it includes the Copilot PII input restriction (closing the gap from Incident 1), correctly narrows the sales prohibition to IP-review rather than a blanket ban (more proportionate and legally grounded), includes the Slack enforcement item, includes budget tracking, and has the most precise incident citations. The DPA citation mismatch present in Proposal 3 is absent.

**Proposal 1** is nearly identical to Proposal 2 in substance (it's essentially the same document with a changelog preamble). The preamble explaining changes is not part of the policy memo itself and shouldn't count against it, but Proposal 2 presents the same content more cleanly as a standalone document.

**Proposal 3** has two clear failures: (1) the citation mismatch in Prohibited Use #2 (DPA cited for a copyright incident), and (2) the new quarterly written report process in Enforcement #4 that violates the "no new tooling/processes" constraint.

## Ranking

RANKING: [2], [1], [3]

I'll evaluate each proposal systematically against the constraints and quality criteria.

---

## Constraint Checklist

**Max 500 words** (body text only, excluding headers/metadata):
- Proposal 1: ~480 words ✓
- Proposal 2: ~400 words ✓ (plus ~300 words of preamble explanation, but body is compliant)
- Proposal 3: ~430 words ✓

**Exactly 4 sections:** All three ✓

**Numbered items in each section:** All three ✓

**Every prohibition references motivating base fact:** 
- P1: ✓ all four prohibitions cited
- P2: ✓ all four prohibitions cited
- P3: Prohibited Use #2 cites "outside counsel DPA finding" — but the motivating fact for that prohibition is Incident 2 (copyright infringement), not DPA terms. The DPA finding concerns customer data in third-party services, not copyright in sales copy. This is a **citation mismatch** ✗

**Enforceable without new tooling:**
- P1: ✓ uses existing PR workflows, supervisory review, Slack admin
- P2: ✓ same
- P3: Enforcement #4 requires managers to "submit a written quarterly report to the CTO" — this is a **new administrative process** not derivable from existing controls ✗

**No aspirational language:**
- P1: ✓ clean
- P2: ✓ clean
- P3: Permitted Use #3: "will be approved" is borderline but acceptable; however "may be approved by the CTO following Legal review" is slightly aspirational ✗ (minor)

---

## Quality Analysis

**Proposal 1** is the most complete and carefully constructed. It explicitly addresses the Copilot input restriction (customer PII/financial data not submitted to Copilot), includes Slack enforcement via IT admin controls, tracks budget enforcement via procurement records, and all citations are accurate. The prohibition citations are correctly matched to their motivating facts.

**Proposal 2** is essentially a corrected and tightened version of Proposal 1, explicitly fixing the problems present in Proposals 1 and 3. It correctly removes the DPA citation from Prohibited Use #2 (fixing the mismatch in P3), removes the new-process quarterly report (fixing P3's enforcement gap), adds the Copilot input restriction, adds Slack enforcement, and adds budget enforcement. It is the most constraint-compliant and logically rigorous version.

**Proposal 3** has two clear constraint violations: the citation mismatch in Prohibited Use #2 (DPA cited instead of copyright finding) and the new administrative process in Enforcement #4 (quarterly written reports). It also lacks explicit Copilot input restrictions and budget enforcement tracking.

---

## Ranking

RANKING: [2], [1], [3]
